evals.name: new-repo-overview
evals.prompt: I just cloned the repo at /home/tank/projects/home/contasty and I'm completely new to it. Give me a high-level tour: what does this project do, and what are the main modules under src/ and their responsibilities? I don't need implementation details yet, just the lay of the land.
evals.expected_output: A concise architecture overview: correctly states contasty strips code bodies/values to produce compact LLM context, and names the real top-level modules under src/ (e.g. main, lib, inputs, query, config, render, stats, lang) with a short responsibility each. No invented modules.
assertions: States the purpose correctly: contasty strips/elides code bodies (and values/long strings) to produce compact context for an LLM/agent
Names at least 4 real src modules from {inputs, query, config, render, stats, lang, walk, main, lib}
Every module/file named under src/ actually exists (no hallucinated modules)
Ran the contasty CLI during exploration (commands.log shows a contasty invocation)

evals.name: library-api-surface
evals.prompt: I want to use the contasty library crate (source at /home/tank/projects/home/contasty/src) as a dependency. What's its public API surface — the main public types and the top-level functions I'd call, with their signatures? I don't care about the internals of each function, just the surface I program against.
evals.expected_output: Lists real public items with accurate signatures: top-level fns like resolve, collect, render_markdown, render_json, and types like StripSet/Category/FileStrip/Stripped plus the stats module. Signatures match the source. No hallucinated exports.
assertions: Lists the real top-level public functions resolve and collect, plus at least one of render_markdown / render_json
Mentions real public types from {StripSet, Category, FileStrip, IgnoreMode, Stripped, Registry, AppError}
Reported signatures match the source (parameters/return types not invented)
No hallucinated public exports (every named item is actually pub in the crate)
Ran the contasty CLI during exploration (commands.log shows a contasty invocation)

evals.name: trace-strip-flow
evals.prompt: In the codebase at /home/tank/projects/home/contasty, trace how the --strip category selection flows from the CLI flag through to where source files actually get stripped. Name the key types and functions on that path. I need the structure, not a deep read of every function body.
evals.expected_output: Correctly traces: CLI --strip (src/main.rs) parsed via StripSet::parse_list, grouped by path_groups, into resolve, then collect (src/walk.rs) applying the StripSet. References real symbols and files that exist in the repo.
assertions: Identifies the --strip flag is parsed into a StripSet (e.g. via StripSet::parse_list)
Names path_groups as the step grouping paths with their strip set in main.rs
Traces through to collect (src/walk.rs) as where files are actually stripped
All referenced files and symbols exist in the repo (no hallucinated paths/functions)
Ran the contasty CLI during exploration (commands.log shows a contasty invocation)
